<!-- Topic 4: Smart Pointers Callback -->
<!-- Total slides: 2 -->

# Smart Pointers Callback

---

## We built toward this in Week 13 {.smaller}

+ Leaks happen when `delete` is forgotten. Dangling pointers happen when memory is used after `delete`.
+ Smart pointers solve both — the destructor calls `delete` automatically when the pointer goes out of scope.

::: notes
Total slides: 2
This is a one-slide bridge back to week 13.02, not a re-teach. Students have seen unique_ptr and shared_ptr. The goal is to connect the problem (leaks, dangling) to the solution they already know.
:::

<!-- Slide 1 -->

---

## unique_ptr and shared_ptr

```{.cpp}
#include <memory>
using namespace std;

// unique_ptr — one owner; deleted when it goes out of scope
unique_ptr<int> p = make_unique<int>(42);
cout << *p << "\n";   // 42
// no delete needed

// shared_ptr — shared ownership; deleted when last owner is gone
shared_ptr<int> a = make_shared<int>(99);
shared_ptr<int> b = a;
// deleted when both a and b go out of scope
```

::: notes
- Use `unique_ptr` when one owner is enough — which is most of the time
- Use `shared_ptr` when multiple owners genuinely share responsibility
- Raw `new`/`delete` is still worth knowing — smart pointers are built on top of it
:::

<!-- Slide 2 -->
